title: README
text:
An ongoing project to create a series of juypter notebooks .qmd files that can be used to randomly generate sample problems and their worked solutions using python
This readme file needs significant updates


Starting with hypothesis testing, individual .qmd files call the stats_project.py file. The bulk of the code was written when I had very little experience with classes in python and much of this should be restructured. Currently, the code is being adapted to use quarto to render accessible .html documents to meet tile II accessibility guidelines.



Each document will produces 30 practice problems in a .html slideshow rendered with revealjs. Each slide is scrollable and contains a single problem. Problems are generated with a randomly determined sample size between 5 and 15 per group. When more than one group, sample size is equivalent between groups. * 08 - Hypothesis_Test_z-Scores.qmd: hypothesis testing with z-scores * 09 - Single-Sample_t-Tests.qmd: one-sample t-tests * 10 - Independent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: independent-samples t-tests * 11 - Dependent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: dependent-samples t-tests * 12 - one-way_ANOVA.qmd: one-way ANOVA * 13 - repeated-measures_ANOVA: repeated-measures ANOVA * 14 - factorial_ANOVA: two-factor ANOVA




One-Way ANOVA
Repeated-Measures ANOVA

seperate defining question from displaying the results

consider adding: class Question: that will hold the question (this might not be necessary)
consider adding: class Assignment: to hold a collection of question objects.

will output a formatted assignment and it’s answer key


add functionality to specify some details when creating the question such as the distribution

add common standardized distributions as options

expand the text of the question, inlcude a list of common variables
switch from slide-based format to a book

combine all into a single book style document.
add instructions and a full walkthrough for each problem type

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {1037} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {19719} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {56}\) 
\(T_{A} = {260} \quad SS_{A} = {115.43}\)  \(T_{B} = {267} \quad SS_{B} = {96.93}\)  \(T_{C} = {220} \quad SS_{C} = {58.86}\)  \(T_{D} = {290} \quad SS_{D} = {62.86}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.85}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {56} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {55}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {56} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {52}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {19719} - \frac{1037^2}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {19719} - \frac{1075369}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {19719} - {19203.02}\]  \[SS_{total} = {515.98}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {115.43 + 96.93 + 58.86 + 62.86}\]  \[SS_{within} = {334.08}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {515.98} - {334.08}\]  \[SS_{between} = {181.9}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {14} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {13}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {52} - {13}\]  \[df_{error} = {39}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{73^2}{4} + \frac{73^2}{4} + \frac{80^2}{4} + \frac{70^2}{4} + \frac{82^2}{4} + \frac{64^2}{4} + \frac{77^2}{4} + \frac{66^2}{4} + \frac{73^2}{4} + \frac{75^2}{4} + \frac{77^2}{4} + \frac{70^2}{4} + \frac{85^2}{4} + \frac{72^2}{4}} - \frac{1037^2}{56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{5329}{4} + \frac{5329}{4} + \frac{6400}{4} + \frac{4900}{4} + \frac{6724}{4} + \frac{4096}{4} + \frac{5929}{4} + \frac{4356}{4} + \frac{5329}{4} + \frac{5625}{4} + \frac{5929}{4} + \frac{4900}{4} + \frac{7225}{4} + \frac{5184}{4}} - \frac{1075369}{56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{1332.25} + {1332.25} + {1600.0} + {1225.0} + {1681.0} + {1024.0} + {1482.25} + {1089.0} + {1332.25} + {1406.25} + {1482.25} + {1225.0} + {1806.25} + {1296.0}} - {19203.02}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {19313.75} - {19203.02}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {110.73}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {334.08} - {110.73}\]  \[SS_{error} = {223.35}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{181.9}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {60.63}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{223.35}{39}\]  \[MS_{error} = {5.73}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{60.63}{5.73}\]  \[F_{obt} = {10.58}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{181.9}{{515.98} - {110.73}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{181.9}{405.25}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.45}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {39}) = {10.58}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.45}\)

\(T_{A} = {357} \quad SS_{A} = {388.1}\)  \(T_{B} = {247} \quad SS_{B} = {492.1}\)  \(T_{C} = {424} \quad SS_{C} = {314.4}\)  \(T_{D} = {344} \quad SS_{D} = {316.4}\)  \(T_{E} = {347} \quad SS_{E} = {272.1}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.89}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {50} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {49}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {50} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {45}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {62481} - \frac{1719^2}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {62481} - \frac{2954961}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {62481} - {59099.22}\]  \[SS_{total} = {3381.78}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {388.1 + 492.1 + 314.4 + 316.4 + 272.1}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1783.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3381.78} - {1783.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1598.68}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {10} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {9}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {45} - {9}\]  \[df_{error} = {36}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{197^2}{5} + \frac{168^2}{5} + \frac{184^2}{5} + \frac{167^2}{5} + \frac{166^2}{5} + \frac{174^2}{5} + \frac{159^2}{5} + \frac{160^2}{5} + \frac{157^2}{5} + \frac{187^2}{5}} - \frac{1719^2}{50}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{38809}{5} + \frac{28224}{5} + \frac{33856}{5} + \frac{27889}{5} + \frac{27556}{5} + \frac{30276}{5} + \frac{25281}{5} + \frac{25600}{5} + \frac{24649}{5} + \frac{34969}{5}} - \frac{2954961}{50}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{7761.8} + {5644.8} + {6771.2} + {5577.8} + {5511.2} + {6055.2} + {5056.2} + {5120.0} + {4929.8} + {6993.8}} - {59099.22}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {59421.8} - {59099.22}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {322.58}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {1783.1} - {322.58}\]  \[SS_{error} = {1460.52}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{1598.68}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {399.67}\]

Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1598.68}{{3381.78} - {322.58}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{1598.68}{3059.2}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.52}\]

\(T_{A} = {83} \quad SS_{A} = {44.86}\)  \(T_{B} = {125} \quad SS_{B} = {8.86}\)  \(T_{C} = {121} \quad SS_{C} = {35.43}\)  \(T_{D} = {119} \quad SS_{D} = {8.0}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.16}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {28} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {27}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {28} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {24}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7428} - \frac{448^2}{28}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7428} - \frac{200704}{28}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7428} - {7168.0}\]  \[SS_{total} = {260.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {44.86 + 8.86 + 35.43 + 8.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {97.15}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {260.0} - {97.15}\]  \[SS_{between} = {162.85}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {6}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {24} - {6}\]  \[df_{error} = {18}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{63^2}{4} + \frac{69^2}{4} + \frac{68^2}{4} + \frac{62^2}{4} + \frac{61^2}{4} + \frac{61^2}{4} + \frac{64^2}{4}} - \frac{448^2}{28}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{3969}{4} + \frac{4761}{4} + \frac{4624}{4} + \frac{3844}{4} + \frac{3721}{4} + \frac{3721}{4} + \frac{4096}{4}} - \frac{200704}{28}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{992.25} + {1190.25} + {1156.0} + {961.0} + {930.25} + {930.25} + {1024.0}} - {7168.0}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {7184.0} - {7168.0}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {16.0}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {97.15} - {16.0}\]  \[SS_{error} = {81.15}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{162.85}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {54.28}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{81.15}{18}\]  \[MS_{error} = {4.51}\]

Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{85.7}{17.48}\]  \[F_{obt} = {4.9}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{342.8}{{1064.0} - {92.0}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{342.8}{972.0}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.35}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {36}) = {4.9}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.35}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{10.14}{19.77}\]  \[F_{obt} = {0.51}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{40.55}{{513.2} - {77.2}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{40.55}{436.0}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {20}) = {0.51}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {21} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {20}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {21} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {18}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {46970} - \frac{958^2}{21}\]  \[SS_{total} = {46970} - \frac{917764}{21}\]  \[SS_{total} = {46970} - {43703.05}\]  \[SS_{total} = {3266.95}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {734.0 + 1359.43 + 842.86}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2936.29}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3266.95} - {2936.29}\]  \[SS_{between} = {330.66}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {6}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {18} - {6}\]  \[df_{error} = {12}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{152^2}{3} + \frac{151^2}{3} + \frac{146^2}{3} + \frac{122^2}{3} + \frac{132^2}{3} + \frac{117^2}{3} + \frac{138^2}{3}} - \frac{958^2}{21}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{23104}{3} + \frac{22801}{3} + \frac{21316}{3} + \frac{14884}{3} + \frac{17424}{3} + \frac{13689}{3} + \frac{19044}{3}} - \frac{917764}{21}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{7701.33} + {7600.33} + {7105.33} + {4961.33} + {5808.0} + {4563.0} + {6348.0}} - {43703.05}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {44087.32} - {43703.05}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {384.27}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {2936.29} - {384.27}\]  \[SS_{error} = {2552.02}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{330.66}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {165.33}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{2552.02}{12}\]  \[MS_{error} = {212.67}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{165.33}{212.67}\]  \[F_{obt} = {0.78}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{330.66}{{3266.95} - {384.27}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{330.66}{2882.68}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {12}) = {0.78}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {21} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {20}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {21} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {18}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53834} - \frac{1056^2}{21}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53834} - \frac{1115136}{21}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53834} - {53101.71}\]  \[SS_{total} = {732.29}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {166.86 + 45.71 + 48.86}\]  \[SS_{within} = {261.43}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {732.29} - {261.43}\]  \[SS_{between} = {470.86}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {7} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {6}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {18} - {6}\]  \[df_{error} = {12}\]

Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{470.86}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {235.43}\]

Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-0.67}{{{\sqrt{8.81}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-0.67}{2.97}\]  \[d = {-0.23}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(14) = -0.87, p > 0.01, d = -0.23

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-3.12 - 0}{1.26}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-3.12}{1.26}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-2.48}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-3.12}{{{\sqrt{12.7}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-3.12}{3.56}\]  \[d = {-0.88}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(7) = -2.48, p < 0.05, d = -0.88

Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{9.28}{7}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{1.33}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {1.15}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{0.57 - 0}{1.15}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{0.57}{1.15}\]  \[t_{obt} = {0.5}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{0.57}{{{\sqrt{9.28}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{0.57}{3.05}\]  \[d = {0.19}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(6) = 0.5, p > 0.01, d = 0.19

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 11\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 11\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{1.96}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{\sqrt{7}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{3}{2.65}\]  \[\sigma_M = {1.13}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{14.86 - 11}{1.13}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{3.86}{1.13}\]  \[z_{obt} = {3.42}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{14.86 - 11}{3}\]  \[d = \frac{3.86}{3}\]  \[d = {1.29}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 3.42, p < 0.05, d = 1.29

Independent-Samples t-test
Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {36.56}, M_B = {55.67}\] \[SS_A = {102.22}, SS_B = {180.0}\] \[n_A = {9}, n_B = {9}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.92}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {16}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{102.22 + 180.0}{8 + 8}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{282.22}{16}\]  \[s_p^2 = {17.64}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{17.64}{9} + \frac{17.64}{9}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.96 + 1.96}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{3.92}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.98}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(36.56 - 55.67) - {0}}{1.98}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-19.11}{1.98}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-9.65}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(36.56 - 55.67)}{{{\sqrt{17.64}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-19.11)}{4.2}\]  \[d = {-4.55}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(16) = -9.65, p < 0.01, d = -4.55

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {3022} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {158762} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {60}\) 
\(T_{A} = {580} \quad SS_{A} = {488.67}\)  \(T_{B} = {749} \quad SS_{B} = {316.92}\)  \(T_{C} = {518} \quad SS_{C} = {1337.67}\)  \(T_{D} = {631} \quad SS_{D} = {798.92}\)  \(T_{E} = {544} \quad SS_{E} = {834.67}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.54}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {60} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {59}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {60} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {55}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {158762} - \frac{3022^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {158762} - \frac{9132484}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {158762} - {152208.07}\]  \[SS_{total} = {6553.93}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {488.67 + 316.92 + 1337.67 + 798.92 + 834.67}\]  \[SS_{within} = {3776.85}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {6553.93} - {3776.85}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2777.08}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{2777.08}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {694.27}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{3776.85}{55}\]  \[MS_{within} = {68.67}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{694.27}{68.67}\]  \[F_{obt} = {10.11}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{2777.08}{6553.93}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.42}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {55}) = {10.11}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.42}\)

Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {27} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {26}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {27} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {24}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18880} - \frac{698^2}{27}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18880} - \frac{487204}{27}\]  \[SS_{total} = {18880} - {18044.59}\]  \[SS_{total} = {835.41}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {142.22 + 134.0 + 416.0}\]  \[SS_{within} = {692.22}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {835.41} - {692.22}\]  \[SS_{between} = {143.19}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {9} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {8}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {24} - {8}\]  \[df_{error} = {16}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{80^2}{3} + \frac{86^2}{3} + \frac{74^2}{3} + \frac{74^2}{3} + \frac{85^2}{3} + \frac{72^2}{3} + \frac{74^2}{3} + \frac{88^2}{3} + \frac{65^2}{3}} - \frac{698^2}{27}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{6400}{3} + \frac{7396}{3} + \frac{5476}{3} + \frac{5476}{3} + \frac{7225}{3} + \frac{5184}{3} + \frac{5476}{3} + \frac{7744}{3} + \frac{4225}{3}} - \frac{487204}{27}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{2133.33} + {2465.33} + {1825.33} + {1825.33} + {2408.33} + {1728.0} + {1825.33} + {2581.33} + {1408.33}} - {18044.59}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {18200.64} - {18044.59}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {156.05}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {692.22} - {156.05}\]  \[SS_{error} = {536.17}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{143.19}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {71.6}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{536.17}{16}\]  \[MS_{error} = {33.51}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{71.6}{33.51}\]  \[F_{obt} = {2.14}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.19}{{835.41} - {156.05}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{143.19}{679.36}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.21}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {16}) = {2.14}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.21}\)

title: Start Here! - Sample Document
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {267} \quad M_{A1B1} = {29.67}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1924.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {388} \quad M_{A1B2} = {43.11}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {886.89} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {461} \quad M_{A1B3} = {51.22}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {223.56} \quad n_{A1B3} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {377} \quad M_{A2B1} = {41.89}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {416.89} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {354} \quad M_{A2B2} = {39.33}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {644.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {376} \quad M_{A2B3} = {41.78}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {1053.56} \quad n_{A2B3} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {98835} - \frac{2223^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {98835} - \frac{4941729}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {98835} - {91513.5}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7321.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1924.0 + 886.89 + 223.56 + 416.89 + 644.0 + 1053.56}\)  \(SS_{within} = {5148.9}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7321.5} - {5148.9}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2172.6}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1116}  \quad n_{A_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1107}  \quad n_{A_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.19}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1116}^2}{27} + \frac{{1107}^2}{27}} - \frac{{2223}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1245456}}{27} + \frac{{1225449}}{27}} - \frac{{4941729}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{46128.0} + {45387.0}} - {91513.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {91515.0} - {91513.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1.5}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.5}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1.5}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.5}{107.27}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.5}{{7321.5} - {1034.78} - {1136.32}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.5}{5150.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.01}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {644}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {742}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {837}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{644}^2}{18} + \frac{{742}^2}{18} + \frac{{837}^2}{18}} - \frac{{2223}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{414736}}{18} + \frac{{550564}}{18} + \frac{{700569}}{18}} - \frac{{4941729}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{23040.89} + {30586.89} + {38920.5}} - {91513.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {92548.28} - {91513.5}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1034.78}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1034.78}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {517.39}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{517.39}{107.27}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {4.82}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1034.78}{{7321.5} - {1.5} - {1136.32}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1034.78}{6183.68}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {4.82}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2172.6} - {1.5} - {1034.78}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1136.32}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1136.32}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {568.16}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{568.16}{107.27}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {5.3}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1136.32}{{7321.5} - {1.5} - {1034.78}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1136.32}{6285.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {5.3}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.18}\)

Problem 1
Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\)

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {21.86}, M_B = {25.86}\] \[SS_A = {138.86}, SS_B = {166.86}\] \[n_A = {7}, n_B = {7}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.18}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {12}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{138.86 + 166.86}{6 + 6}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{305.72}{12}\]  \[s_p^2 = {25.48}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{25.48}{7} + \frac{25.48}{7}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{3.64 + 3.64}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{7.28}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {2.7}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(21.86 - 25.86) - {0}}{2.7}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-4.0}{2.7}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-1.48}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(21.86 - 25.86)}{{{\sqrt{25.48}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-4.0)}{5.05}\]  \[d = {-0.79}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(12) = -1.48, p > 0.05, d = -0.79

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{3.17}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {10}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{130.0 + 173.33}{5 + 5}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{303.33}{10}\]  \[s_p^2 = {30.33}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{30.33}{6} + \frac{30.33}{6}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{5.06 + 5.06}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{10.12}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {3.18}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(45.0 - 39.67) - {0}}{3.18}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{5.33}{3.18}\]  \[t_{obt} = {1.68}\]

The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(10) = 1.68, p > 0.01, d = 0.97

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.76}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {28}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{1214.93 + 4013.33}{14 + 14}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{5228.26}{28}\]  \[s_p^2 = {186.72}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{186.72}{15} + \frac{186.72}{15}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{12.45 + 12.45}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{24.9}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {4.99}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(51.93 - 39.67) - {0}}{4.99}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{12.26}{4.99}\]  \[t_{obt} = {2.46}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(51.93 - 39.67)}{{{\sqrt{186.72}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(12.26)}{13.66}\]  \[d = {0.9}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(28) = 2.46, p > 0.01, d = 0.9

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {764} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {31794} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {20}\) 
\(T_{A} = {158} \quad SS_{A} = {573.2}\)  \(T_{B} = {209} \quad SS_{B} = {258.8}\)  \(T_{C} = {158} \quad SS_{C} = {117.2}\)  \(T_{D} = {239} \quad SS_{D} = {698.8}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.24}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {20} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {19}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {20} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {16}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {31794} - \frac{764^2}{20}\]  \[SS_{total} = {31794} - \frac{583696}{20}\]  \[SS_{total} = {31794} - {29184.8}\]  \[SS_{total} = {2609.2}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {573.2 + 258.8 + 117.2 + 698.8}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1648.0}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2609.2} - {1648.0}\]  \[SS_{between} = {961.2}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{961.2}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {320.4}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{1648.0}{16}\]  \[MS_{within} = {103.0}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{320.4}{103.0}\]  \[F_{obt} = {3.11}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{961.2}{2609.2}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.37}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {16}) = {3.11}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.37}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {16323} - \frac{747^2}{36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {16323} - \frac{558009}{36}\]  \[SS_{total} = {16323} - {15500.25}\]  \[SS_{total} = {822.75}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {135.67 + 210.67 + 154.25}\]  \[SS_{within} = {500.59}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {822.75} - {500.59}\]  \[SS_{between} = {322.16}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{322.16}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {161.08}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{500.59}{33}\]  \[MS_{within} = {15.17}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{161.08}{15.17}\]  \[F_{obt} = {10.62}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{322.16}{822.75}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.39}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {33}) = {10.62}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.39}\)

\(T_{A} = {336} \quad SS_{A} = {490.4}\)  \(T_{B} = {319} \quad SS_{B} = {562.9}\)  \(T_{C} = {374} \quad SS_{C} = {626.4}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {30} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {29}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {2}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {30} - {3}\]  \[df_{within} = {27}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {37133} - \frac{1029^2}{30}\]  \[SS_{total} = {37133} - \frac{1058841}{30}\]  \[SS_{total} = {37133} - {35294.7}\]  \[SS_{total} = {1838.3}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {490.4 + 562.9 + 626.4}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1679.7}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1838.3} - {1679.7}\]  \[SS_{between} = {158.6}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{158.6}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {79.3}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{1679.7}{27}\]  \[MS_{within} = {62.21}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{79.3}{62.21}\]  \[F_{obt} = {1.27}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{158.6}{1838.3}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.09}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {27}) = {1.27}, p > {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.09}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {24342} - \frac{1192^2}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {24342} - \frac{1420864}{60}\]  \[SS_{total} = {24342} - {23681.07}\]  \[SS_{total} = {660.93}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {140.93 + 189.73 + 135.33 + 80.4}\]  \[SS_{within} = {546.39}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {660.93} - {546.39}\]  \[SS_{between} = {114.54}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]

Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{114.54}{660.93}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.17}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {3.91}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.17}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 1
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {461} \quad M_{A1B1} = {51.22}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1381.56} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {433} \quad M_{A1B2} = {48.11}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {594.89} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {530} \quad M_{A1B3} = {58.89}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {388.89} \quad n_{A1B3} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {454} \quad M_{A2B1} = {50.44}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {466.22} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {280} \quad M_{A2B2} = {31.11}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {786.89} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {251} \quad M_{A2B3} = {27.89}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {1002.89} \quad n_{A2B3} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {451} \quad M_{A3B1} = {50.11}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {950.89} \quad n_{A3B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {355} \quad M_{A3B2} = {39.44}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {726.22} \quad n_{A3B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {400} \quad M_{A3B3} = {44.44}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {426.22} \quad n_{A3B3} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {81} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {80}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {81} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {72}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {175375} - \frac{3615^2}{81}\)  \(SS_{total} = {175375} - \frac{13068225}{81}\)  \(SS_{total} = {175375} - {161336.11}\)  \(SS_{total} = {14038.89}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1381.56 + 594.89 + 388.89 + 466.22 + 786.89 + 1002.89 + 950.89 + 726.22 + 426.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = {6724.67}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {14038.89} - {6724.67}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7314.22}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1424}  \quad n_{A_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {985}  \quad n_{A_2} = {27}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1206}  \quad n_{A_3} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1424}^2}{27} + \frac{{985}^2}{27} + \frac{{1206}^2}{27}} - \frac{{3615}^2}{81}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2027776}}{27} + \frac{{970225}}{27} + \frac{{1454436}}{27}} - \frac{{13068225}}{81}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{75102.81} + {35934.26} + {53868.0}} - {161336.11}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {164905.07} - {161336.11}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {3568.96}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{3568.96}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1784.48}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1784.48}{93.4}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {19.11}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3568.96}{{14038.89} - {1676.51} - {2068.75}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3568.96}{10293.63}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {19.11}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1366}  \quad n_{B_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1068}  \quad n_{B_2} = {27}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1181}  \quad n_{B_3} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1366}^2}{27} + \frac{{1068}^2}{27} + \frac{{1181}^2}{27}} - \frac{{3615}^2}{81}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1865956}}{27} + \frac{{1140624}}{27} + \frac{{1394761}}{27}} - \frac{{13068225}}{81}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{69109.48} + {42245.33} + {51657.81}} - {161336.11}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {163012.62} - {161336.11}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {1676.51}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{1676.51}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {838.26}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{838.26}{93.4}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {8.97}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1676.51}{{14038.89} - {3568.96} - {2068.75}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1676.51}{8401.18}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.2}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {8.97}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.2}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.59}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {7314.22} - {3568.96} - {1676.51}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2068.75}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2068.75}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {517.19}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{517.19}{93.4}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {5.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2068.75}{{14038.89} - {3568.96} - {1676.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2068.75}{8793.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {72}) = {5.54}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 2
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {95} \quad M_{A1B1} = {19.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {74.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {122} \quad M_{A1B2} = {24.4}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {99.2} \quad n_{A1B2} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {101} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.2}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {36.8} \quad n_{A2B1} = {5}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {114} \quad M_{A2B2} = {22.8}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {254.8} \quad n_{A2B2} = {5}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {20} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {19}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {20} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {16}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9886} - \frac{432^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9886} - \frac{186624}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9886} - {9331.2}\)  \(SS_{total} = {554.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {74.0 + 99.2 + 36.8 + 254.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {464.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {554.8} - {464.8}\)  \(SS_{between} = {90.0}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {217}  \quad n_{A_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {215}  \quad n_{A_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{217}^2}{10} + \frac{{215}^2}{10}} - \frac{{432}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{47089}}{10} + \frac{{46225}}{10}} - \frac{{186624}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{4708.9} + {4622.5}} - {9331.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {9331.4} - {9331.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {0.2}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{0.2}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {0.2}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{0.2}{29.05}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.2}{{554.8} - {80.0} - {9.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.2}{465.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.01}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {196}  \quad n_{B_1} = {10}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {236}  \quad n_{B_2} = {10}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{196}^2}{10} + \frac{{236}^2}{10}} - \frac{{432}^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{38416}}{10} + \frac{{55696}}{10}} - \frac{{186624}}{20}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{3841.6} + {5569.6}} - {9331.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {9411.2} - {9331.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {80.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{80.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {80.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{80.0}{29.05}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.75}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.0}{{554.8} - {0.2} - {9.8}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{80.0}{544.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {2.75}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.15}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.49}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {90.0} - {0.2} - {80.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {9.8}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{9.8}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {9.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{9.8}{29.05}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.34}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.8}{{554.8} - {0.2} - {80.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.8}{474.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {0.34}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 3
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {216} \quad M_{A1B1} = {36.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {36.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {224} \quad M_{A1B2} = {37.33}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {5.33} \quad n_{A1B2} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {114} \quad M_{A2B1} = {19.0}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {152.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {6}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {151} \quad M_{A2B2} = {25.17}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {88.83} \quad n_{A2B2} = {6}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {24} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {23}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {24} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {20}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22387} - \frac{705^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22387} - \frac{497025}{24}\)  \(SS_{total} = {22387} - {20709.38}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1677.62}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {36.0 + 5.33 + 152.0 + 88.83}\)  \(SS_{within} = {282.16}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1677.62} - {282.16}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1395.46}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {440}  \quad n_{A_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {265}  \quad n_{A_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{440}^2}{12} + \frac{{265}^2}{12}} - \frac{{705}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{193600}}{12} + \frac{{70225}}{12}} - \frac{{497025}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{16133.33} + {5852.08}} - {20709.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {21985.41} - {20709.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1276.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1276.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1276.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1276.03}{14.11}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {90.43}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1276.03}{{1677.62} - {84.37} - {35.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1276.03}{1558.19}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.82}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {90.43}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.82}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {330}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {375}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{330}^2}{12} + \frac{{375}^2}{12}} - \frac{{705}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{108900}}{12} + \frac{{140625}}{12}} - \frac{{497025}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{9075.0} + {11718.75}} - {20709.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {20793.75} - {20709.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {84.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{84.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {84.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{84.37}{14.11}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.98}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{84.37}{{1677.62} - {1276.03} - {35.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{84.37}{366.53}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.23}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {5.98}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.23}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1395.46} - {1276.03} - {84.37}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {35.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{35.06}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {35.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{35.06}{14.11}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.48}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{35.06}{{1677.62} - {1276.03} - {84.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{35.06}{317.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {2.48}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {105310} - \frac{2800^2}{78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {105310} - \frac{7840000}{78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {105310} - {100512.82}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4797.18}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {673.23 + 590.31 + 238.31 + 157.08 + 449.08 + 767.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2875.7}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4797.18} - {2875.7}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1921.48}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {972}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {882}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {946}  \quad n_{A_3} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{972}^2}{26} + \frac{{882}^2}{26} + \frac{{946}^2}{26}} - \frac{{2800}^2}{78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{944784}}{26} + \frac{{777924}}{26} + \frac{{894916}}{26}} - \frac{{7840000}}{78}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{36337.85} + {29920.15} + {34419.85}} - {100512.82}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {100677.85} - {100512.82}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {165.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{165.03}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {82.52}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{82.52}{39.94}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.07}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{62.82}{39.94}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {1.57}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.82}{{4797.18} - {165.03} - {1693.63}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{62.82}{2938.52}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {72}) = {1.57}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1921.48} - {165.03} - {62.82}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1693.63}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1693.63}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {846.82}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{846.82}{39.94}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {21.2}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1693.63}{{4797.18} - {165.03} - {62.82}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1693.63}{4569.33}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.37}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {21.2}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.37}\)

Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1000}  \quad n_{A_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {916}  \quad n_{A_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {785}  \quad n_{A_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1000}^2}{24} + \frac{{916}^2}{24} + \frac{{785}^2}{24}} - \frac{{2701}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1000000}}{24} + \frac{{839056}}{24} + \frac{{616225}}{24}} - \frac{{7295401}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{41666.67} + {34960.67} + {25676.04}} - {101325.01}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {102303.38} - {101325.01}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {978.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{978.37}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {489.18}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{489.18}{62.31}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {7.85}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{978.37}{{5885.99} - {19.02} - {776.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{978.37}{5090.97}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {7.85}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1369}  \quad n_{B_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1332}  \quad n_{B_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.04}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1369}^2}{36} + \frac{{1332}^2}{36}} - \frac{{2701}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1874161}}{36} + \frac{{1774224}}{36}} - \frac{{7295401}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{52060.03} + {49284.0}} - {101325.01}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {101344.03} - {101325.01}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {19.02}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{19.02}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {19.02}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{19.02}{{5885.99} - {978.37} - {776.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{19.02}{4131.62}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {0.31}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1773.39} - {978.37} - {19.02}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {776.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{776.0}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {388.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{388.0}{62.31}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.23}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{776.0}{{5885.99} - {978.37} - {19.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{776.0}{4888.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {6.23}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {25425} - \frac{1077^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {25425} - \frac{1159929}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {25425} - {24165.19}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1259.81}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {208.0 + 104.88 + 78.88 + 155.5 + 54.0 + 37.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {639.14}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1259.81} - {639.14}\)  \(SS_{between} = {620.67}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {351}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {373}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {353}  \quad n_{A_3} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.22}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{351}^2}{16} + \frac{{373}^2}{16} + \frac{{353}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1077}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{123201}}{16} + \frac{{139129}}{16} + \frac{{124609}}{16}} - \frac{{1159929}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{7700.06} + {8695.56} + {7788.06}} - {24165.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {24183.68} - {24165.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {18.49}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{18.49}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {9.24}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{9.24}{15.22}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.61}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{18.49}{{1259.81} - {0.02} - {602.16}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{18.49}{657.63}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {0.61}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {539}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {538}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{539}^2}{24} + \frac{{538}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1077}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{290521}}{24} + \frac{{289444}}{24}} - \frac{{1159929}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{12105.04} + {12060.17}} - {24165.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {24165.21} - {24165.19}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {0.02}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.02}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {0.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{0.02}{15.22}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.0}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.02}{{1259.81} - {18.49} - {602.16}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.02}{639.16}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {42}) = {0.0}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.22}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {620.67} - {18.49} - {0.02}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {602.16}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{602.16}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {301.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{301.08}{15.22}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {19.78}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{602.16}{{1259.81} - {18.49} - {0.02}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{602.16}{1241.3}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {19.78}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38928} - \frac{1094^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38928} - \frac{1196836}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {38928} - {37401.12}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1526.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {203.88 + 104.88 + 239.5 + 175.5}\)  \(SS_{within} = {723.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1526.88} - {723.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = {803.12}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {582}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {512}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{582}^2}{16} + \frac{{512}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1094}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{338724}}{16} + \frac{{262144}}{16}} - \frac{{1196836}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{21170.25} + {16384.0}} - {37401.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {37554.25} - {37401.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {153.13}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{153.13}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {153.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{153.13}{25.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {5.92}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{153.13}{{1526.88} - {2.0} - {647.99}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{153.13}{876.89}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {5.92}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {543}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {551}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{543}^2}{16} + \frac{{551}^2}{16}} - \frac{{1094}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{294849}}{16} + \frac{{303601}}{16}} - \frac{{1196836}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{18428.06} + {18975.06}} - {37401.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {37403.12} - {37401.12}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {2.0}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.0}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {2.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.0}{25.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.08}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.0}{{1526.88} - {153.13} - {647.99}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.0}{725.76}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.08}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.2}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {803.12} - {153.13} - {2.0}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {647.99}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{647.99}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {647.99}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{647.99}{25.85}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {25.07}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{647.99}{{1526.88} - {153.13} - {2.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{647.99}{1371.75}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {25.07}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.47}\)
